summary: Add ESLint strict-TypeScript configuration and npm lint script
problem_statement: The codebase lacks automated static analysis. Without ESLint enforcing strict TypeScript rules, type-unsafe patterns (widespread 'any', implicit returns, unhandled promises) go undetected until they cause runtime failures. Every subsequent R2 refactoring phase depends on a linter baseline to prevent regressions.
in_scope: Install ESLint with the typescript-eslint plugin and strict type-checked preset
Create an ESLint configuration file at the project root targeting the application source
Add an npm run lint script that runs ESLint across the application codebase
Fix or annotate any ESLint errors in currently-touched application code so the script exits cleanly on the R2 baseline
out_of_scope: Formatting rules — handled by Prettier in P1-R2-02
Pre-commit hook wiring — handled in P1-R2-03
Fixing lint errors in code not touched by R2
Adding custom rules beyond the strict type-checked preset
requirements: ESLint is installed as a dev dependency with typescript-eslint strict type-checked config
npm run lint executes ESLint and returns exit code 0 on the current codebase
No 'any'-permissive overrides are added globally — individual exceptions must use inline eslint-disable with justification
acceptance_criteria: npm run lint passes with zero errors for the application codebase in scope for R2
ESLint configuration targets TypeScript strict rules
The lint script is documented in package.json scripts
dependencies: None — this is the first task in the R2 sequence
risks: Strict preset may surface a large number of pre-existing violations requiring triage of fix-now vs suppress-with-comment
implementation_notes: Use flat config (eslint.config.js). Align parser options with the existing tsconfig. The strict type-checked preset is preferred over the base preset to match the epic's goal of strict TypeScript enforcement.
